events.title: The Minoan civilization flourishes on Crete
The Mycenaean civilization rises on the Greek mainland
The traditional date for the Trojan War
Bronze Age collapse begins the Greek Dark Ages
Greek city-states, or poleis, begin to emerge
The first Olympic Games are held at Olympia
The Greek alphabet develops from Phoenician script
Draco writes a harsh new law code for Athens
Solon reforms Athenian law
Peisistratus becomes tyrant of Athens
Cleisthenes establishes democracy in Athens
The Battle of Marathon
The Battles of Thermopylae and Salamis
The Battle of Plataea ends the Persian invasion
The Age of Pericles begins in Athens
Construction begins on the Parthenon
The Peloponnesian War begins
Sparta defeats Athens, ending the Peloponnesian War
Socrates is tried and executed
Plato founds the Academy in Athens
Philip II becomes king of Macedon
Macedon defeats the Greek city-states at Chaeronea
Alexander the Great becomes king
Alexander the Great dies
Rome conquers Greece
